meta.title: Wave 6 — OpenClaw Absorption Pass
meta.description: 6 features. Smallest wave — mostly platform. Gateway framing + onboarding ceremony are the targets.

props.title: Gateway Absorption
props.subtitle: OpenClaw is a product, not a framework. 3 steal, 1 wrap, 1 reject, 1 extract. The gateway-as-control-plane framing is the architectural win.

props.content: OpenClaw's 40+ connector-skills are tool bindings (Notion, Slack, WhatsApp, etc.) — NOT lifecycle skills. Its value for Lev is the **gateway-as-control-plane** framing (typed WS + compatible HTTP on one surface), the onboarding wizard ceremony, and the remote access playbook. The channel/plugin/app sprawl stays behind — Lev is a runtime, not a personal assistant product.

rows.feature: Gateway as typed WS control plane + OpenAI-compatible HTTP

props.title: 🌐 → /arch (Gateway Pattern)
props.content:
**Absorb:** Gateway-as-control-plane framing

OpenClaw runs typed WebSocket for real-time control + OpenAI-compatible HTTP for standard API consumers on ONE gateway process. This is the strongest conceptual model for Lev's eventual gateway surface.

**Section to add:** 'Gateway Control Plane Pattern (from OpenClaw WS+HTTP convergence)'

props.title: 🧭 → /agent-field-kit (Onboarding + Remote)
props.content:
**Absorb:** Consumer-grade setup + remote access playbook

oc-02: `openclaw onboard` flow — provider selection, channel setup, first message test. The ceremony that makes new users feel the product is real.
oc-04: Tailscale/SSH/trusted-proxy remote access. Docker setup at ~/claws/.

**Section to add:** 'Consumer Onboarding Flow (from OpenClaw)' + 'Remote Access Playbook (from OpenClaw Tailscale)'

props.title: ✅ → /lev-align (Doctor overlap)
props.content:
**Absorb:** Doctor/repair/migration surface

oc-03 overlaps with Hermes hm-02 — both have doctor commands. The combined absorption into /lev-align gives: runtime health check (Hermes-style profile-aware) + repair/migration (OpenClaw-style actionable remediation). One consumer health shell, two inspiration sources.

props.title: oc-05: Security model → wrap only
props.content: OpenClaw has sandbox + tool policy. Lev already has AgentGuard + prompt-guard + dcg. Wrap the concepts (boundary awareness), don't replace the implementation.

props.title: oc-06: Channel/plugin/app surface → reject
props.content: Voice, canvas, WhatsApp, Telegram, Signal, Discord, Slack, iMessage, SMS, email... OpenClaw is a personal assistant product. Lev is a runtime. Product-surface sprawl doesn't belong in Lev core. Period.

**Wave 1 (AXI):** 9 patterns → /ux + /tribunal. Published.
**Wave 2 (OOO + OMX):** 14 patterns → 8 skills. Published.
**Wave 4 (Clawhip):** 11 features — 4 absorb→skills, 4 absorb→lev-tmux, 2 reject. Published.
**Wave 5 (Hermes):** 9 features — 5 steal→skills, 2 extract, 1 reject. Published.
**Wave 6 (OpenClaw):** 6 features — 3 steal→skills, 1 wrap, 2 reject. Published.

**Total across all waves:** ~49 features evaluated, ~35 absorbed into ~12 Lev skills, ~8 deferred to Wave 3 lev-tmux, ~6 rejected.

**Next: Tribunal review across all waves, then ship the skill doc edits.**

props.title: Phase 6: Tribunal Review
props.content: Run /tribunal on waves 1-6. 3-model QA: is each absorption truly net-new? Is the target skill the right home? Any misclassifications?

props.title: Phase 4a: Ship Skill Edits
props.content: Edit ~12 skill docs with absorption sections. Source attribution to parity files.

props.title: Phase 4b: Update Parity
props.content: Mark absorbed features with action: absorbed + absorbed_into: /skill + wave: N
